channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rhode Island man says he was falsely accused of defacing patriotic crosswalk
item.description: "They were wrong from the start and they should pay," Michael Dellefratte told NewsNation affiliate WPRI.
item.dc:creator: Eli Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Forced assimilation and abuse: How US boarding schools devastated Native American tribes
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — The White House says President Joe Biden will apologize on behalf of the U.S. government Friday for its 150-year campaign to break up Native American culture, language and identity by forcing children into abusive Indian boarding schools.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida man killed dog because power wasn't restored after Milton, sheriff says
item.description: A Florida man was arrested after he allegedly shot and killed his dog because he was mad that his power hadn't been restored after Hurricane Milton, according to the sheriff.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, ABC 4

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds New York's ban on guns in sensitive places
item.description: A federal appeals court in New York says a recent Supreme Court decision does not alter its conclusion that New York can enforce laws banning firearms in sensitive locations.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris leaning on some of the biggest names in Democratic politics to close out campaign
item.description: Washington (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris is enlisting some of her most high-profile surrogates in the closing days of the campaign, hoping those names will help energize her supporters to vote early in the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: DAN MERICA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ex-officer on trial in shooting death of Andre Hill thought his life was in danger, attorney says
item.description: A white former Ohio police officer mistakenly shot and killed Andre Hill as the Black man was emerging from a garage nearly four years ago because he saw a flash of light from keys in Hill’s hand and thought he was about to be shot, the officer’s attorney said Thursday during opening statements of h
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: IDs of victims killed in Houston helicopter crash released
item.description: The identities of all four victims killed in Sunday night’s helicopter crash in Houston’s East End have been released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Taylor, Deven Clarke, KPRC 2

item.title: President Biden to apologize for 150-year Indian boarding school policy
item.description: President Joe Biden is preparing to issue an historic apology during his first diplomatic visit to a tribal nation as president on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Graham Lee Brewer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: World on pace for significantly more warming without immediate climate action, report warns
item.description: The world is on a path to get 1.8 degrees Celsius (3.2 Fahrenheit) warmer than it is now, but could trim half a degree of that projected future heating if countries do everything they promise to fight climate change, a United Nations report said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: State senator dies following freak lawn mower accident
item.description: Kentucky state Sen. Johnnie Turner died more than a month after a lawn mower accident. U.S. Senate Minority Leader Mitch McConnell issued a statement about Turner.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kentucky lawmaker dies weeks after lawnmower plunges into swimming pool
item.description: Kentucky state Sen. Johnnie Turner (R) died Tuesday from injuries he suffered after being thrown into an empty swimming pool while riding his lawnmower just over a month ago.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beyoncé expected to join Harris at Houston rally: Reports
item.description: Beyoncé is poised to get in "Formation" behind Vice President Kamala Harris' White House bid. The music superstar will reportedly be joining the vice president at a Friday campaign rally in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: Kouri Richins appeals to reconsider bail and release from custody
item.description: The Utah mom's attorneys say she has been a model inmate and the wait for trial has gone on for too long.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation

item.title: Lifelong 'rivals' recall love and admiration for pitching legend Fernando Valenzuela
item.description: "Nobody will ever do what he did, we lost a living legend, he was me, he was you, he was our brothers, sisters, cousins, he was us," said Hernandez. "He represented us, he looked like us, talked like us, acted like us, and the loss is for every single one of us.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Police charge 16-year-old with murder in death of girl in Austin
item.description: Austin Police said a 16-year-old boy was arrested in connection with the Monday death of a 16-year-old girl. The boy has been charged with murder.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia's Gullah-Geechee community seeks path forward after deadly dock collapse
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — The partial collapse of a ferry dock that claimed the lives of seven people attending a cultural festival has left a historic Black community in Georgia struggling with how to move forward.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The 3 men convicted of murdering Ahmaud Arbery motion for new trial
item.description: Travis and Gregory McMichael armed themselves with guns and jumped in a pickup truck to chase Arbery, a 25-year-old Black man, after they saw him run past their house on Feb. 23, 2020.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DNC hits Trump over reported praise of Hitler's generals in mobile billboard
item.description: The Democratic National Committee (DNC) is deploying a mobile billboard ahead of former President Donald Trump’s rally in Las Vegas on Thursday, hitting him over his past reported praise of Adolf Hitler’s generals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida sues Garland for blocking Trump assassination attempt investigation
item.description: Florida sued Attorney General Merrick Garland on Wednesday, saying the Justice Department is improperly blocking the state from investigating the second assassination attempt of former President Trump. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DNA links suspected serial killer to 19-year-old's cold case murder in Illinois
item.description: Authorities have linked a suspected serial killer to the kidnapping and murder of a 19-year-old Illinois woman, more than 45 years after her death.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, Fox 5

item.title: Should elephants have the same rights as people? A court in Colorado may decide
item.description: Colorado's highest court will hear arguments on whether the African female elephants should be legally able to challenge their captivity under a long-held process used by prisoners to dispute their detention.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida mailman stabs lawncare worker over parking: deputies
item.description: The Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office has released more information about the events that may have led up to a deadly stabbing Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, ABC 4

item.title: Ron Ely, TV's 'Tarzan' in the 1960s, dies at 86
item.description: Ron Ely, the tall, musclebound actor who played the title character in the 1960s NBC series “Tarzan,” has died at age 86.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Evangelicals emerge as a potent lobby for US support of Ukraine
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Since the war in Ukraine began, Yaroslav Pyzh, a Baptist pastor in Lviv, has worn many hats. He runs the large and growing Ukrainian Baptist Theological Seminary, while overseeing a network of 18 humanitarian aid centers across the embattled country.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Colleges left helpless as students rule out schools due to state politics
item.description: Colleges and universities find themselves increasingly trapped with the politics of the state where they reside.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Should elephants have the same rights as people? A court in Colorado may decide
item.description: A court will hear arguments on whether the some elephants should be legally able to challenge their captivity.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: No end for Boeing labor strike as workers reject latest contract proposal
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Boeing factory workers voted against the company’s latest contract offer and remain on the picket lines six weeks into a strike that has stopped production of the aerospace giant’s bestselling jetliners.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG and MANUEL VALDES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Should elephants have the same rights as people? A Colorado court may decide
item.description: Colorado’s highest court is considering whether five zoo elephants can challenge their confinement under a legal process mainly used by prisoners to dispute their detention.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Slevin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Funeral home owners accused of storing decaying bodies expected to plead guilty to COVID-19 fraud
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Colorado funeral home owners accused of misspending nearly $900,000 in COVID-19 pandemic relief funds and living lavishly, all while allegedly stashing 190 decaying bodies in a building and sending grieving families fake ashes, are expected to plead guilty to federal charges Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The 2024 election is major source of stress: Survey
item.description: A total of 72% worry that the election results could lead to violence, and 56% believe the election could lead to the end of democracy in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: DNA helps Illinois police make breakthrough in 1970s cold case murder of 19-year-old
item.description: An Illinois police department announced it cracked a cold case homicide from the 1970s through DNA testing. It said 19-year-old Kathy Halle was murdered by serial killer Bruce Lindahl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cash App founder's ex-wife slams alleged killer sister's testimony
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Bob Lee's ex-wife did not hold back her feelings toward the accused killer's sister at the courthouse on Wednesday. "My husband was murdered. She has no right to make herself a victim in this.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Man wrongly convicted of murder starts new job after 28 years behind bars
item.description: “I knew they have a good reputation of training individuals getting ready for the workforce,” Jones said. He had two sons who completed the program.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Bernthal, NewsNation

item.title: Pennsylvania high court gives voters provisional option if their mail ballots get rejected
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Pennsylvania's highest court on Wednesday said people whose mail ballots are rejected for not following technical procedures in state law can cast provisional ballots, a decision sure to affect some of the thousands of mail-in votes likely to be rejected this fall.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former 'American Idol' hopeful arrested on child porn charges
item.description: Police say they found hundreds of images and videos of child pornography on a smartphone while searching his home.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Richardson/ KFOR, KTLA 5

item.title: A man who threatened to kill Democratic election officials pleads guilty
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A Colorado man repeatedly made online threats about killing the top elections officials in his state and Arizona — both Democrats — as well as a judge and law enforcement agents, according to a guilty plea he entered Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal appeals panel says suspended lawyer Michael Avenatti should be resentenced
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — A federal appeals panel has vacated a 14-year sentence for suspended lawyer Michael Avenatti, who rose to fame representing porn actor Stormy Daniels against former President Donald Trump and was later convicted of bilking his California clients.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
